search_schema.name: search_schema
search_schema.description: Explore schema by natural language. Returns relevant object types, relation types, action types, metric types, condition operations, and data source metadata for downstream query tools.

query_object_instance.name: query_object_instance
query_object_instance.description: Query instances of one known object type with filters, sorting, selected properties, and cursor-style pagination. Use this for row-level object retrieval, not joins or aggregation.

query_instance_subgraph.name: query_instance_subgraph
query_instance_subgraph.description: Fetch instance subgraphs by following relation paths across object types. Use this when the question needs related objects around one or more seed instances.

get_logic_properties_values.name: get_logic_properties_values
get_logic_properties_values.description: Resolve calculated logic properties, such as metrics or operator-backed properties, for a batch of object instances.

get_action_info.name: get_action_info
get_action_info.description: Return executable action definitions for object instances, including dynamic tool schemas that an agent can call.

find_skills.name: find_skills
find_skills.description: Find available skills that match a knowledge network, object type, instance context, or user intent.

list_knowledge_networks.name: list_knowledge_networks
list_knowledge_networks.description: List knowledge networks visible to the current account. Call this first to obtain kn_id for other tools.

get_kn_detail.name: get_kn_detail
get_kn_detail.description: Get full knowledge network details, including concept groups, object types, relation types, and action types. Use this when a known kn_id needs a complete schema snapshot.

run_sql.name: run_sql
run_sql.description: Run read-only Trino SQL against data resources mounted by a knowledge network. Use table placeholders such as {{.resource_id}} and physical column names.

list_resources.name: list_resources
list_resources.description: List visible data-layer resources independent of ontology object types. Use this when resources are not modeled as object types or when querying raw data directly.

describe_resource.name: describe_resource
describe_resource.description: Describe one data resource's physical schema, including connector type and columns. Use this before writing run_sql against raw resources.
